user_prompt: read `lib\FlyGui\readme.md` and implement the design, make sure to comment where something is implemented according to a part of my design
agent_reply: - fast vs slow poll mode, with slow mode capped at 15 Hz
- top bar drawing for RTC date/time and battery
- sibling image-buffer lookup to avoid duplicate RAM loads
- partial redraw of changed character ranges
I also added the requested per-screen files under `lib/FlyGuiViews`, one class/file pair per view, starting at [MainScreenView.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/MainScreenView.h:5).
I added comments prefixed with `Design:` at the places where code directly implements a part of your readme design.
- A targeted `pio ci` compile did compile the new FlyGui sources cleanly; its final link failed only because the temporary CI input was missing unrelated app demo sources called by `main.cpp`.

user_prompt: In `ErrorView`, move the sprite and the message text down by 20 pixels, as it is conflicting with the top bar
also you need to signal to the top bar to do a full refresh instead of a partial text refresh, because you used `display.fillScreen(TFT_BLACK);`.
also, you have written some text drawing helper functions that might be reused, move those into `lib\FlyGui\FlyGuiText.cpp` and `lib\FlyGui\FlyGuiText.h`
agent_reply: - Moved the ErrorView icon down from `y=4` to `y=24`.
- Moved the wrapped error message down from `y=120` to `y=140`.
- `ErrorView` now requests a full top-bar redraw after `display.fillScreen(TFT_BLACK)`.
- `FlyGui::redraw()` now immediately redraws the top bar again if a view invalidates it mid-redraw.

- Moved the word-wrap helper into `FlyGuiTextUtil::drawWrappedText(...)` in [FlyGuiText.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.h) and [FlyGuiText.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp).
- Moved `sprite_desc_t` into [IconLookup.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/SpriteDraw/IconLookup.h), since `inc/sprites.h` is generated and got overwritten during build.

commit_message: good progress working on GUI but ran into memory problems with PNG decode

user_prompt: avoid having `M5GFX& display` as a parameter for functions and methods, there should be a global reference to the display for anything unrelated to FlyGui, and if FlyGui is involved, there should be a way to dig through trees to get a reference to it, or just use the global reference
agent_reply: - Updated direct display users to use `thefly_display` instead of threading a local `display` reference or calling `M5.Display` directly.
- Kept `FlyGui::display()` as a convenience getter that returns the global display, so tree-based GUI code can still ask the GUI for its screen if needed.

user_prompt: the rendering of the text for caller info, it's not using a fixed width font, so the rendering leaves artifacts that we assumed would've been overwritten
that chunk of text should either use a fixed width font, or be totally cleared on update by a black rectangle
agent_reply: Fixed. I added `FlyGuiText::setClearOnUpdate(true)` and enabled it only for `callerInfoText_` in `RecordingView`.
So that caller-info line now clears its full black rectangle before drawing the next rotating value, while the rest of the text system can keep its lighter partial redraw behavior.
Fixed. I added `FlyGuiText::setClearOnUpdate(true)` and enabled it only for `callerInfoText_` in `RecordingView`.
So that caller-info line now clears its full black rectangle before drawing the next rotating value, while the rest of the text system can keep its lighter partial redraw behavior.

diagnostics, fixed a bug with speaker peak decay

user_prompt: the top of the screen will say something like "2026-06-24 01:23:45" as the clock
make it so that if the power key is held down, it starts to say "2026-06-24 01:23:45 Z" then "2026-06-24 01:23:45 ZZ" then "2026-06-24 01:23:45 ZZZ", updating every second
don't actually enforce a count for the number of Z
of course, if the key is released, display no Z, probably by a full refresh call
agent_reply: Implemented the top-bar power-key hold cue.

- [FlyGui.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:153) now asks the clock item to update power-key hold state right after `M5.update()`, and forces a top-bar redraw on hold/release transitions.
- [FlyGuiText.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp:286) now appends ` Z`, ` ZZ`, ` ZZZ`, etc. once per held second.
No shutdown/count enforcement was added; it is purely visual.

One hardware wrinkle: Core2’s AXP192 power key is exposed by M5Unified as PMIC short/long events, not a clean continuous GPIO press. So this uses true press/release when available, and falls back to `M5.BtnPWR.wasHold()` on the PMIC path.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`: build passes.
Implemented the top-bar power-key hold cue.

- [FlyGui.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:153) now asks the clock item to update power-key hold state right after `M5.update()`, and forces a top-bar redraw on hold/release transitions.
- [FlyGuiText.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp:286) now appends ` Z`, ` ZZ`, ` ZZZ`, etc. once per held second.
No shutdown/count enforcement was added; it is purely visual.

One hardware wrinkle: Core2’s AXP192 power key is exposed by M5Unified as PMIC short/long events, not a clean continuous GPIO press. So this uses true press/release when available, and falls back to `M5.BtnPWR.wasHold()` on the PMIC path.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`: build passes.

user_prompt: also, it's now not registering the release, the list of Z just keeps growing even though the key is released
agent_reply: The issue was that `M5.BtnPWR.wasHold()` on the PMIC path is a one-shot event, not a true “currently held” state, and it doesn’t reliably pair with a release event. I changed that fallback so it self-clears after 1 second instead of latching forever, while keeping the real `isPressed()/wasPressed()` path as the proper immediate hold/release path.

Build passed. One caveat: on Core2/AXP192, M5Unified still does not appear to expose a true live PMIC power-key down/up state, so exact “first Z on physical press, clear on physical release” likely needs a lower-level AXP192 IRQ/status implementation rather than `BtnPWR.wasHold()`.

The issue was that `M5.BtnPWR.wasHold()` on the PMIC path is a one-shot event, not a true “currently held” state, and it doesn’t reliably pair with a release event. I changed that fallback so it self-clears after 1 second instead of latching forever, while keeping the real `isPressed()/wasPressed()` path as the proper immediate hold/release path.

Build passed. One caveat: on Core2/AXP192, M5Unified still does not appear to expose a true live PMIC power-key down/up state, so exact “first Z on physical press, clear on physical release” likely needs a lower-level AXP192 IRQ/status implementation rather than `BtnPWR.wasHold()`.

user_prompt: now it is showing a single Z, and then it disappears
also it's not reacting instantly like I wanted
agent_reply: You’re right. The single-Z behavior was my fallback being too fake, so I removed the PMIC `wasHold()` path entirely.
Now the Z indicator only uses a real live button state:
- release clears via the normal release/click events

Important caveat: on Core2/AXP192, M5Unified’s `BtnPWR` does not expose instant physical press/release for the PMIC power key. It only gives delayed classified events like `wasClicked()` / `wasHold()`. So this removes the misleading delayed Z, but true instant Z on Core2 still needs lower-level verified AXP192 PEK edge/status handling, or a real GPIO signal for the key.

user_prompt: You know how the RecordingView has a text area for the call meta data? That meta data is now kind of useless since we are no longer writing metadata into the files.
If the MicGainManager is in the high gain mode, then make that text area display in red text "earbud mic too weak", stop the display of the call meta data
This should be responsive to hot-plug and hot-unplug
This should work for both bluetooth recording and local memo recording
agent_reply: RecordingView now checks `MicGainManager::inlineMicMaximumGainMode()` in [RecordingView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:535). When high-gain mode is active, the caller-info text area switches to red and shows:

While that warning is active, call metadata is not displayed. When high-gain clears, such as hot-unplug resetting the inline mic mode, the field returns to white; Bluetooth recording resumes caller metadata immediately, and memo recording clears the field.
I also added `FlyGuiText::setTextColor()` in [FlyGuiText.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp:176) so the existing text widget can redraw correctly when only the color changes.

RecordingView now checks `MicGainManager::inlineMicMaximumGainMode()` in [RecordingView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:535). When high-gain mode is active, the caller-info text area switches to red and shows:

While that warning is active, call metadata is not displayed. When high-gain clears, such as hot-unplug resetting the inline mic mode, the field returns to white; Bluetooth recording resumes caller metadata immediately, and memo recording clears the field.
I also added `FlyGuiText::setTextColor()` in [FlyGuiText.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp:176) so the existing text widget can redraw correctly when only the color changes.

commit_message: mic gain manager has a max gain setting

user_prompt: I made a mistake in my instruction, I copied in the wrong enum, correct instructions should be `while in the EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_W_WEAK_MIC, it behaves exactly like EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD`
ok I did a live test with everything enabled, it's still not loud enough
we need to undo some stuff
keep the detection for the weak mic
but we will not have a high gain mode, remove any unused tuning constants

instead, the ExtCodec will have a new state that is `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_W_WEAK_MIC`, only `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_WITH_MIC` can transition to `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_W_WEAK_MIC`, and `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_W_WEAK_MIC` cannot transition into `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_WITH_MIC`

while in the `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_W_WEAK_MIC`, it behaves exactly like `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD`, except, the error message `earbud mic too weak` is shown. In fact, that message is now two lines, `earbud mic too weak\nusing built-in mic`
agent_reply: - Removed the high-gain mode and unused max-gain tuning constants from [conf.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:103) and `MicGainManager`.
- Added `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_W_WEAK_MIC`; only `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_WITH_MIC` can mark itself weak, and weak will not transition back to `WITH_MIC` until unplug/replug.
- Weak state now routes like `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD`: line-in/right built-in mic path, earbud present, full duplex available.
- `RecordingView` now shows red two-line text: `earbud mic too weak\nusing built-in mic`, for both Bluetooth and local memo recording.
- Added multiline support to `FlyGuiText` so that warning renders properly.
Build passed. I also checked that the old high-gain symbols are gone. Unrelated dirty files already in the worktree were left alone.
